segmentsJSON: [{"text":"Hello and welcome to The Rest is Politics US with me, Cady Kay.","end":11.66,"start":0},{"text":"And me, Anthony Scaramucci. Good morning, Cady.","end":14.780000000000001,"start":11.92},{"text":"So, Anthony, how are you spending Monday evening? The only thing, by the way, for all of our","end":20.22,"start":14.98},{"text":"listeners around the world that anyone in the States is interested in right now, and particularly","end":25.3,"start":20.22},{"text":"anyone in New York City is interested in right now, is... Well, I'm going to the Knick final,","end":30.04,"start":25.3},{"text":"but look, Trump's going also to the Knick final. So, he's got the entire city on lockdown.","end":36.480000000000004,"start":30.34},{"end":40.02,"start":37.4,"text":"So, c'est la vie. Raining on New York's parade."},{"end":45.58,"start":40.22,"text":"Yeah, it's all good. No problem. I didn't want my kids to miss it. So, I bought the tickets."},{"end":48.22,"start":45.68,"text":"We're ready to go. Let's go New York. Everyone's going to be excited."},{"end":50.54,"start":48.22,"text":"But in the meantime... Yes."},{"end":56.82,"start":50.72,"text":"Are we fighting... Cady, tell me how you define a ceasefire. The president says, well,"},{"end":61.76,"start":57.36,"text":"you know, a ceasefire on the other side of the world may or may not be a ceasefire. A few bombs"},{"end":65.98,"start":61.76,"text":"here and there. I thought that was just a very interesting way of saying other people do things"},{"end":70.94,"start":65.98,"text":"differently from how we do them. Tell me how do you define a ceasefire and do... Are we in a ceasefire"},{"end":76.86,"start":70.94,"text":"right now, Cady Kay? I think what matters is the countries that are fighting, not the geographical"},{"end":81.76,"start":76.86,"text":"location of the ceasefire. I'm just going to posit it a bit like that. So, if the United"},{"end":86.62,"start":81.76,"text":"States is fighting, whether it's in the Middle East or in Latin America or in the United States"},{"end":91,"start":86.62,"text":"or in Asia, a ceasefire is a ceasefire is a ceasefire and this doesn't seem to be one."},{"end":95.84,"start":91,"text":"So, we're going to be talking about that in the first half of the program and whether Donald"},{"end":102.36,"start":95.84,"text":"Trump has just boxed himself in in such a way that he's got to try and put pressure on Israel."},{"end":105.9,"start":102.62,"text":"He desperately doesn't want to get back into a fighting match with Iran."},{"end":111.68,"start":105.9,"text":"His options, his roadmap for getting out of this thing, a lot of those exits seem to be"},{"end":115.78,"start":111.68,"text":"blocked at the moment and they're blocked by decisions of his own making. In the second"},{"end":120.66000000000001,"start":115.78,"text":"half, we're going to talk about Donald Trump's interview with Meet the Press on NBC that ended"},{"end":127.82000000000001,"start":120.66000000000001,"text":"in a walkout after a rather contentious debate over, yes, the 2020 election and the January"},{"end":128.58,"start":127.82000000000001,"text":"the 6th riot."},{"end":133.24,"start":128.92000000000002,"text":"Good. I want to talk about that with you because you're the media person. I just play a media"},{"end":139.52,"start":133.24,"text":"person on this podcast, but you're the media person. I really want to understand NBC, CBS,"},{"end":147.10000000000002,"start":140.52,"text":"mainstream news media. Where are things going in this country and around the world?"},{"end":154.88,"start":147.10000000000002,"text":"And what is the discussion about how to handle Donald Trump and why is it that he has now been"},{"end":162.1,"start":154.88,"text":"on our radars since 2015? But I still think we haven't quite figured out how to handle somebody"},{"end":168.56,"start":162.1,"text":"who routinely lies to journalist faces. Okay, so let's start though with what is happening in the"},{"end":173.62,"start":168.56,"text":"Middle East and this ceasefire that is not a ceasefire or maybe it is a ceasefire, but it depends on how"},{"end":179.82,"start":173.62,"text":"much pressure Iran is going to put on Israel and how much pressure Trump is going to put on Israel as"},{"end":186.34,"start":179.82,"text":"well. So where do you think we are, Anthony? Because I think this is one of those moments where"},{"end":193.32,"start":186.34,"text":"we seem to have rode back from a huge amount of escalation. We had this situation where the"},{"end":200.1,"start":193.32,"text":"Israelis hit Beirut. The Iranians had signaled they wouldn't tolerate that. So the Iranians hit"},{"end":209.18,"start":200.1,"text":"Israel. Israel then retaliated against Iran, but in a fairly limited way after Donald Trump called"},{"end":214.64000000000001,"start":209.18,"text":"Benjamin Netanyahu and says, do not burn Tehran to the ground, which is what some Israeli politicians"},{"end":220.96,"start":214.64000000000001,"text":"were asking for and calling for. And I thought it was such an interesting moment where you had Donald"},{"end":228.32,"start":220.96,"text":"Trump making major international policies that affected his America's allies. But how was he doing"},{"end":233.88,"start":228.32,"text":"it? He was doing it by answering journalists phone calls again. Every time there seems to be a moment of"},{"end":238.74,"start":233.88,"text":"tension in the Middle East and things flare up again, Donald Trump, of course, gets a string of phone"},{"end":244.34,"start":238.74,"text":"calls from reporters in Washington, D.C. And what does he do? He tells those reporters what he is"},{"end":251.28,"start":244.34,"text":"about to tell Benjamin Netanyahu. So Netanyahu hears it from the Financial Times or from Axios,"},{"end":258.40000000000003,"start":251.8,"text":"exactly what Trump is going to do. And I think that it's symbolic of the kind of chaos in the decision"},{"end":265.08,"start":258.40000000000003,"text":"making. This is malpractice from a diplomatic standpoint to be answering phone calls and announcing"},{"end":269.41999999999996,"start":265.08,"text":"your policy to journalists before you've told your ally what the policy is going to be."},{"end":274.2,"start":270.03999999999996,"text":"But that is perhaps not the only thing we need to take away from this incident. What did you make of"},{"end":282.34,"start":274.2,"text":"it? For people that want to really understand Donald Trump, whatever the norms are, I'm above those norms."},{"end":288.24,"start":282.34,"text":"And so it doesn't matter. So if the norms are talking to my allies quietly, talking to my adversaries"},{"end":293.92,"start":288.24,"text":"quietly, well, I'm not going to do that because those are the norms. And so I am telling you,"},{"end":299.28000000000003,"start":294.02,"text":"everybody prior to me is a dummy and every procedure and protocol that's happened before me"},{"end":306.76,"start":299.28000000000003,"text":"is ill-advised and dumb. And so only I am the person that's able to do things. And so therefore,"},{"end":313.26,"start":306.76,"text":"I'll do the things exactly the way I want them, whatever I want them. And your job is to be in the"},{"end":319.36,"start":313.26,"text":"sidecar with me and do exactly what I want when I want them. And so that's the biggest problem here."},{"end":326.64,"start":319.36,"text":"But I want to go to the Israelis for a second because the Israelis are definitely not on Trump's"},{"end":332.74,"start":326.64,"text":"page. And you know that and I know that. And so the Israelis are like, we're being threatened by"},{"end":339.21999999999997,"start":332.74,"text":"Hezbollah in the North from Lebanon. These guys want to take us out with a nuclear weapon,"},{"end":347.28000000000003,"start":339.22,"text":"likely. The prime minister of Israel, we can debate his strategy because I think he's hurt the Israeli"},{"end":353.22,"start":347.28000000000003,"text":"brand. If I'm being honest, that's not anti-Semitic to say that. Okay. I'm a pro-Semite, but I think"},{"end":359.40000000000003,"start":353.22,"text":"he's hurt the Israeli brand because of the way he's approaching things. There's no delicacy to what he's"},{"end":365.08000000000004,"start":359.40000000000003,"text":"doing. And so the Israeli position right now, he's going to back the deal, but he's still going to shoot"},{"end":372.32,"start":365.08,"text":"missiles into Lebanon. And that's not going to work, Adi. So for me, you're not going to get a"},{"end":377.28,"start":372.32,"text":"Hezbollah carve out of this thing. You're either going to have a ceasefire and the stuff's going to"},{"end":382.28,"start":377.28,"text":"end. But I think Netanyahu is in a terrible position because I think he's boxed in from both sides."},{"end":388.58,"start":382.65999999999997,"text":"The opposition leader is accusing Netanyahu of failing to secure Israel's demands as part of the"},{"end":395.46,"start":388.58,"text":"ceasefire. That's Yair Lapid. He's got the ultra-Orthodox coalition, which has been with him"},{"end":401.3,"start":395.46,"text":"the whole time. Super mad at him because they feel like, believe it or not, he's been too weak on this"},{"end":407.71999999999997,"start":401.3,"text":"stuff. So reconcile for me, the relationship between Benjamin Netanyahu and Donald Trump"},{"end":416.47999999999996,"start":407.71999999999997,"text":"and the triangulation with Iran and why, and again, I'll just state my opinion first, I don't think they"},{"end":421.06,"start":416.48,"text":"get to a deal anytime soon. No, I think, okay. So I think that's the one thing we can take away from"},{"end":427.04,"start":421.06,"text":"this is that there is not a deal coming anytime soon. And we can get to oil markets reactions to"},{"end":433.86,"start":427.04,"text":"all of this in a second, because I do want to ask you about that. But I think Iran has imposed a new"},{"end":442.40000000000003,"start":433.86,"text":"equation on the region just in the last 48 hours. Trump has made it completely clear that he has run out"},{"end":448.28,"start":442.4,"text":"of patience with fighting. He has said as much, I was getting bored. The whole process was getting"},{"end":454.84,"start":448.28,"text":"boring. He wants this whole thing over. And the message there to Iran right now, after that phone"},{"end":461.02,"start":454.84,"text":"call that Donald Trump had with Netanyahu yesterday saying, do not strike back, do not make this another"},{"text":"big escalation. The message to Tehran, you can strike Israel without retaliation, without the risk of","end":468.76,"start":461.02},{"text":"Israel retaliating against you because Donald Trump will protect you. At the moment, American gas prices","end":476.4,"start":468.76},{"text":"are shielding Iran from retaliation by Israel. That is a phenomenal turn of events. Imagine if that","end":485.65999999999997,"start":476.4},{"text":"had been Barack Obama. Imagine if Barack Obama had protected the Ayatollahs against America's number","end":493.82,"start":485.65999999999997},{"text":"one ally in the region, Israel, because of high gas prices. Because that's effectively what happened","end":500.48,"start":493.82},{"text":"in the last 24 hours. Let's go to that thing, because I think this is very important. Why is Trump","end":506.08,"start":500.48},{"text":"treated differently than Barack Obama? Because if I listed everything that Trump has done, and I said,","end":512.7,"start":506.08},{"text":"Barack Obama did these things, Washington would be on fire. Washington would be on fire.","end":517.76,"start":512.76},{"text":"Yeah. And I would say actually, and it's pointed out very often by the Obama team,","end":522.28,"start":517.76},{"text":"Barack Obama was America's first black president, and his room for maneuver was much more limited","end":528.54,"start":523.06},{"text":"in terms of what acceptable from the mainstream, from the press, from the Republican Party. And I think","end":537.42,"start":528.54},{"text":"there's some justification to that. But I don't know that it was just Barack Obama. I chose Barack Obama","end":543.26,"start":537.42},{"text":"there. But if Joe Biden had done that, he would have been hammered as well, particularly by the","end":549.24,"start":543.26},{"text":"Republican Party. And the real biggest difference is, it's not just the press. It's more that Donald","end":556.48,"start":549.24},{"text":"Trump controls the Republican Party. And so even though you have hawks in the Republican Party who","end":562.8199999999999,"start":556.48},{"text":"are appalled at what they're seeing, you have staunch Israel allies in the Republican Party who are","end":570.52,"start":562.8199999999999},{"text":"appalled at the idea that Israel does not have a right to defend itself in this situation,","end":575.3,"start":570.52},{"text":"Donald Trump owns the Republican Party. That's the single biggest difference. The single most","end":580.76,"start":576.1},{"text":"powerful driver here is that Donald Trump owns his party.","end":584.18,"start":580.76},{"text":"Okay. I want to take you into the trip US situation room.","end":588.0799999999999,"start":584.46},{"text":"Oh, good. Okay.","end":588.9,"start":588.24},{"text":"Okay. And now we're going to-","end":590.36,"start":588.9399999999999},{"text":"Are we both being promoted? Do we have like buttons and stuff we can press?","end":593.4,"start":590.36},{"text":"You're the one that's promoted. Okay, no one's letting me near any buttons, Katya. Okay.","end":597.46,"start":593.5},{"text":"Ask my wife. I'm not allowed near any buttons, not even the ignition.","end":601.2,"start":597.46},{"text":"You're barely allowed to the Knicks game this evening without escort.","end":604.2800000000001,"start":601.4000000000001},{"text":"Not even allowed to the ignition to my car. But we're in the situation room. We're in the","end":608.6600000000001,"start":604.4200000000001},{"text":"trip US situation room. And I'm going to posit something and then I need you now to make the","end":615.0600000000001,"start":608.6600000000001},{"text":"decisions. Okay, ready?","end":616.14,"start":615.0600000000001},{"text":"Yeah.","end":616.96,"start":616.7800000000001},{"text":"So we are sitting here. Iran attacked Israel. Israel attacked Iran. That's this morning's","end":623,"start":616.96},{"text":"news. And then Iran announces that they're done attacking Israel.","end":626.32,"start":623},{"text":"Yeah.","end":626.8,"start":626.66},{"text":"Right? You saw that.","end":627.58,"start":626.8},{"text":"Okay. So now I am going to posit to you that there's a 50 to 55% chance that we have a limp","end":635.06,"start":627.98},{"text":"along deal where we get to a perpetually fragile non-war. So it's sort of like a MOU frozen","end":643.5,"start":635.06},{"text":"transactional relationship where they go for 60 days without fighting. And I'm going to now say to","end":650.78,"start":643.5},{"text":"you three things, you tell me if you agree with any of them because I was working on this this","end":653.76,"start":650.78},{"text":"morning. Iran, everybody that I talk to on Wall Street, all the analysts in the Middle East that","end":659.72,"start":653.76},{"text":"I'm close to, they are economically battered. Okay? They definitely need to sell oil.","end":665.8399999999999,"start":659.72},{"text":"Okay?","end":666.4399999999999,"start":666.3399999999999},{"text":"Yes.","end":666.52,"start":666.5},{"text":"So they cannot afford a real war at this point if they're just going to be honest with everybody.","end":670.92,"start":666.52},{"text":"Trump also needs oil to be under $100. Okay? He's got to get this inflation down","end":676.0799999999999,"start":671.52},{"text":"before the midterms and they're feeling the pinch. So for those two reasons, I do think","end":682.86,"start":676.08},{"text":"we are in a quagmire, but we're in sort of a stalemate, which could reduce prices. But here's","end":691.1,"start":682.86},{"text":"the spoiler, Caddy, and here's the question. Israel's a spoiler. It's the one party that's","end":697.96,"start":691.1},{"text":"been restrained here rather than empowered. And they've got a political tug of war going on","end":704.48,"start":697.96},{"text":"inside the country about what they should do and how they should deviate from the United States.","end":709.76,"start":704.48},{"text":"And so what would you recommend to President Trump to get Israel on side?","end":716.2,"start":710.5600000000001},{"text":"Trump is running out of options in the region and Iran has exposed his lack of power in the region.","end":725.26,"start":717.26},{"text":"But the one lever of real power that he does still have in this conflict is his ability,","end":731.78,"start":725.26},{"text":"is to restrain Israel. Now, by calling up journalists and telling them what he's doing,","end":736.6999999999999,"start":731.78},{"text":"he's kind of giving Iran a live feed of American decision-making process during a shooting war.","end":743.16,"start":737.4399999999999},{"text":"He's also burning some bridges with Benjamin Netanyahu. But Benjamin Netanyahu can't,","end":751.5,"start":743.4},{"text":"Mr. President, actually do very much at all without American support. It needs American search","end":758.6,"start":752},{"text":"rescue. It needs American refueling. It needs American logistics. So when Donald Trump tells","end":766.0600000000001,"start":758.6},{"text":"the Financial Times, my friend Ed Luce at the Financial Times, who's one of those journalists","end":770.94,"start":766.0600000000001},{"text":"who's got his numbers and just calls him up, I call all the shots. In a way, Donald Trump is right.","end":778.72,"start":770.94},{"text":"Yes, there was a small retaliation, but it was a kind of kabuki retaliation. America calls the shots","end":785.6600000000001,"start":779},{"text":"when it comes to Israel. For Netanyahu, this is a disaster situation because he's looking at the","end":790.92,"start":785.66},{"text":"choice between staying out of prison by being re-elected. It is existential for him. He's got","end":798.92,"start":790.92},{"text":"to win that election. But in order to win that election, he's got to be tough on Iran. And yet,","end":804.68,"start":798.92},{"text":"there is Donald Trump who is so desperate not to have a resumption of fighting that he is saying,","end":810.92,"start":804.74},{"text":"you can't do that, which means that Netanyahu could be pushed into a position where he has to admit","end":816.0999999999999,"start":810.92},{"text":"electoral defeat at home. Because Trump has made it very clear he's not going to resume this fighting.","end":820.9599999999999,"start":816.0999999999999},{"text":"And he's not going to let Israel screw it up for him.","end":824.18,"start":821.18},{"text":"So, if I want to manipulate Trump, because what we know now is that he's calling journalists and he's","end":830.7199999999999,"start":824.18},{"text":"free-forming and effectively laundering the strategies of the American government. And so,","end":837.9399999999999,"start":830.7199999999999},{"text":"if I want to manipulate Trump, how do I do that? Because what's happening here-","end":845.5999999999999,"start":838},{"text":"If you're Netanyahu, you mean?","end":846.88,"start":845.5999999999999},{"text":"Netanyahu, yeah. Even the Iranians. But he's handing the counterparties,","end":851.9399999999999,"start":847.02},{"start":851.94,"text":"both Netanyahu and Iran, frankly, a free read on his psychology. So, now,","end":859.1600000000001},{"start":859.2,"text":"how would you manipulate him if you could?","end":860.96},{"start":861.3800000000001,"text":"Well, first of all, I wouldn't have him speaking to journalists. I mean, I really wouldn't. I don't","end":865.6600000000001},{"start":865.6600000000001,"text":"think that- He's going to do that anyway.","end":866.84},{"start":866.8800000000001,"text":"He's going to do that anyway.","end":867.7600000000001},{"start":867.7600000000001,"text":"Mr. President, I'd like you to stop speaking to journalists. You're hurting our ability to get","end":871.58},{"start":871.58,"text":"the peace. F you and you're fired after 11 days. I'm going to continue to speak to journalists.","end":877.0400000000001},{"start":877.04,"text":"Okay. So, but then what would you do? Recognizing that you have Trump in power and you're recognizing","end":883.9},{"start":883.9,"text":"that this is the disaster that is Donald Trump. So, what would you do?","end":887.7199999999999},{"start":887.8399999999999,"text":"I think you need to get to two things, right? You need to create space for the negotiations to succeed.","end":895.86},{"start":896.56,"text":"And that's going to mean a couple of things. One is that the Iranians are going to have to have","end":902.76},{"start":902.76,"text":"some kind of upfront payment. Like you say, they're running out of money. They need money now.","end":909.04},{"start":909.2,"text":"Sanctions relief won't cut it. The return on that is going to take too long. They need some","end":913.3},{"start":913.3,"text":"assets unfrozen. So, you need Trump to get to a position where he can describe unfrozen assets","end":923.4},{"start":923.4,"text":"as being the very opposite of what Barack Obama did when he gave Iran unfrozen assets.","end":931},{"start":931,"text":"You're going to have to trick him into thinking there is a way to sell unfrozen assets, Iranian assets,","end":938.46},{"start":938.78,"text":"back to Iran as a win. Because without that, I don't see how he gets a deal.","end":944.12},{"start":944.78,"text":"And he really wants a deal. The trouble is he's gone around telling everyone who will listen in","end":949.3},{"start":949.3,"text":"Washington, including members of Congress I've spoken to, that the one thing he doesn't want is","end":953.78},{"start":953.78,"text":"money going to the Iranians upfront. He's going to have to swallow that. That has to be on the table","end":959.2},{"start":959.2,"text":"if you want to get some kind of a deal. Otherwise, diplomacy is not going to work. So, he's going to","end":963.0600000000001},{"start":963.0600000000001,"text":"have to be able to present himself and you're going to have to give him the language to present","end":967.8000000000001},{"start":967.8000000000001,"text":"himself as the master negotiator who managed to get the Iranians to reopen the Strait of Hormuz","end":974.6600000000001},{"start":974.6600000000001,"text":"for very little money that was theirs anyway while we got access to the nuclear dust that they had","end":982.86},{"start":982.86,"text":"already agreed to water down.","end":984.26},{"start":984.26,"text":"Well, I mean, for me, okay, I'm going to put myself in the Israeli position here for a second,","end":989.48},{"start":989.62,"text":"recognize everything I know about Trump. I would go to Trump and say, listen, I'll trade the Lebanon front.","end":995.4},{"start":996.02,"text":"You want the deal. I get that. You're telling everybody no nuclear weapons, no nuclear weapons,","end":1001.04},{"start":1001.6,"text":"nuclear dust, blah, blah. I would say, hey, I'll trade the front, the Hezbollah strikes,","end":1008.08},{"start":1008.08,"text":"for more definition and more delineation on getting the nuclear material out of that country,","end":1016.4000000000001},{"start":1016.94,"text":"which ironically we had under Obama. But let me just explain because if I can get it out of the","end":1022.38},{"start":1022.38,"text":"country, my position inside of Israel is I'm eliminating the nuclear threat. We're always","end":1030.06},{"start":1030.06,"text":"going to be at war here. Our neighbors and us, for whatever reason, since 1948 are fighting each","end":1036.06},{"start":1036.06,"text":"other, but I don't want to get wiped off the map. And so for me, that would be the move. There's","end":1041.8799999999999},{"start":1041.8799999999999,"text":"440 kilograms of uranium, and I would definitely push for that.","end":1049.08},{"start":1049.56,"text":"And you'd find a way to make Trump accept that he's going to have to pay some money up front.","end":1053.04},{"end":1056.74,"text":"I mean, he's going to have to pay to get the straight open. That's just what it is,","start":1053.26},{"end":1057.84,"text":"and he's going to have to accept that.","start":1056.76},{"end":1065.36,"text":"Yeah. Well, he hates that because he watched Fox News 10, 12 years ago. And when Obama was doing","start":1057.84},{"end":1072,"text":"that, that they were all aghast at that. But that's the real politic, Caddy. They all would","start":1065.36},{"end":1078.8,"text":"tell you this, Kennedy, Eisenhower, Roosevelt. Churchill would tell you this. There's only so much","start":1072},{"end":1085.02,"text":"power that we have, and there's only so much influence that we have on the rest of the world.","start":1078.8},{"end":1092.78,"text":"And whether you're Von Metternich or Henry Kissinger, you pick it, you have to balance the powers.","start":1085.02},{"end":1099.1,"text":"And you have to put everybody in a position where they have a slight political win, even your","start":1093.22},{"end":1107.5,"text":"adversary. Okay. And there's been a few unconditional surrenders, the Nazis in 1945, the Confederate","start":1099.1},{"end":1115.56,"text":"Army in 1865. But that's not this. Okay. There's not going to be Trump called for an unconditional","start":1107.5},{"end":1121.54,"text":"surrender of Iran. But good luck, unless you're going to put 1 million ground troops on the ground","start":1115.56},{"end":1127.6,"text":"and you're going to bomb them into the Stone Age, which nobody has the will for, that's also not","start":1121.54},{"end":1136.08,"text":"going to happen. So to me, if I'm Trump, I'm trying to get the deal done, I'm going to pay the money,","start":1127.6},{"end":1140.56,"text":"even if it's something I don't like or don't want to do.","start":1136.08},{"end":1145.96,"text":"So I think the other thing that I would do on our situation room, our little rest is politics","start":1140.98},{"end":1153.06,"text":"situation room, there would be a big sign on the door, Anthony saying, Lindsey Graham, Ted Cruz,","start":1145.96},{"end":1161.3,"text":"you are not welcome here. Because the Hawks, whilst there is no deal, the Hawks are finding space,","start":1153.9199999999998},{"end":1169.02,"text":"whether it's Hawks in Tehran or Hawks in Washington or Hawks in Jerusalem, the Hawks are finding space","start":1161.3},{"end":1176.18,"text":"to push for escalation. And that's why we need a deal done soon, because they are circling and they","start":1169.02},{"end":1179.56,"text":"are the ones that are trying to ramp this up again, because they don't want the deal.","start":1176.18},{"end":1185.9199999999998,"text":"So we need to get the deal done, which means Trump swallowing a bit of humble pie and realizing what he","start":1180.2},{"end":1191.8000000000002,"text":"has to do. But I don't think, Anthony, I think you and I both agree, it sounds like this morning,","start":1185.92},{"end":1198.48,"text":"I don't think we are anywhere near a deal. Even though President Trump has done his usual,","start":1192.8600000000001},{"end":1204.66,"text":"let's juice the Monday morning markets thing of posting on Truth Social that a deal is very close,","start":1198.8200000000002},{"end":1209.98,"text":"by my reckoning, that is like the fifth iteration of this thing of America, of Trump saying that the","start":1204.76},{"end":1214.6200000000001,"text":"deal is close, and then the deal is nowhere near close, and it all falls apart, and the Iranians walk","start":1209.98},{"end":1218.84,"text":"away. I agree, but he doesn't need, he's going to say that because that's Donald Trump,","start":1214.62},{"end":1225.8799999999999,"text":"but he doesn't need the deal, Caddy. What he needs is the freeze. What he needs is the stalemate,","start":1219.36},{"end":1232.4799999999998,"text":"and what he needs is to let them sell some oil and get some of our Goff ally tankers through the","start":1226.4399999999998},{"end":1234.04,"text":"strait. Yeah, he needs some opening up of the strait.","start":1232.4799999999998},{"end":1234.12,"text":"That's what he needs.","start":1234.04},{"end":1238.62,"text":"But that is, at this point, Anthony, that's the deal. I mean, nobody's thinking that there's going","start":1234.4399999999998},{"end":1243.8,"text":"to be some big nuclear deal announced. All we want, the priority at the moment is to get","start":1238.62},{"end":1247.3999999999999,"text":"the straight open. Okay, here's my question for you before we go to break.","start":1243.8},{"end":1257.18,"text":"At what point does the smart investor money start realizing that when Donald Trump says that there","start":1248.12},{"end":1262.62,"text":"is a deal near, he's probably hiding something, and we are not near a deal?","start":1257.18},{"end":1268.98,"text":"Okay, so it's a great question, but you have to understand how the markets work. The markets are","start":1263.36},{"end":1273.3,"text":"working off of the notion that Trump is going to pump the market on Monday.","start":1268.98},{"end":1275.02,"text":"Exactly what he did this morning.","start":1273.76},{"end":1280.48,"text":"So traders are getting up on Sunday. Remember, I've got buddies of mine. They sit at these huge","start":1275.44},{"end":1288.7,"text":"trading turrets, and they have literally screens dedicated to Trump's truth social. And they have,","start":1280.48},{"end":1294.3600000000001,"text":"you know, bling, bling, bling notifications the minute Trump says something on his truth social.","start":1288.8},{"end":1300.02,"text":"But, you know, the fever and the circuit that you're seeing right now is not going to break","start":1294.36},{"end":1304.9599999999998,"text":"anytime soon because they're trading off of Trump's information the same way he is.","start":1300.02},{"end":1310.34,"text":"You see what I mean? And that's just people saying, okay, and that's unbelievable amounts","start":1305.54},{"end":1315.8,"text":"of cynicism, but that's just the way it works, the way it works in the markets. And so people getting","start":1310.34},{"end":1322.1999999999998,"text":"up on Monday, and it's too late. They got to get up on Sunday, and they've got to look at what the hell's","start":1315.8},{"end":1327.46,"text":"going on and get ready for the Tokyo market to open, Hong Kong market to open, and trade the Trump","start":1322.2},{"end":1332.1000000000001,"text":"tweets. And that's what they've been doing. But oil was up, right, in the Asian markets,","start":1327.46},{"end":1339.3400000000001,"text":"because they've seen what's happening. Yeah. But I don't think we have any resolution to this","start":1332.28},{"end":1347.02,"text":"for several months. But I do think you're going to get the straight open or partially open,","start":1339.3400000000001},{"end":1353.86,"text":"and you're going to get the oil prices into an equipoise. And I think you're going to have a cold","start":1347.02},{"end":1360.46,"text":"peace on this situation, but you're not going to have a resolution because Trump doesn't want to pay","start":1353.86},{"end":1368.2,"text":"the money because of the tarring that he'll get that he's Obama-lite. Netanyahu doesn't really want it","start":1360.46},{"end":1375.22,"text":"to 100% stop either because of the political coalition that's fracturing before him. And the Iranians","start":1368.2},{"end":1379.78,"text":"don't care. They just want to be able to sell oil so they can try to get their economy revived.","start":1375.22},{"end":1386.54,"text":"And so if you take those three points of the triangle, that's a 60-day freeze,","start":1380.48},{"end":1391.5,"text":"let some oil through the straight, and we'll be in this sort of weird equipoise.","start":1386.96},{"end":1396.74,"text":"I had a conversation over the weekend with an international economist who had been studying","start":1391.74},{"end":1403.52,"text":"the Red Sea and the Strait of Hormuz. And he was saying that he doesn't think the Strait of Hormuz","start":1396.74},{"end":1409.6,"text":"is ever going to go back to the kind of transport flows that it was before the war. Because if you","start":1403.52},{"end":1416.12,"text":"look at what happened in the Red Sea when the Houthis started bombing tankers, hitting tankers in the Red","start":1409.6},{"end":1422,"text":"Sea, actually the traffic has never picked up fully even when they stopped doing so. And I think that","start":1416.12},{"end":1431.26,"text":"the legacy that Trump has given the world of this war, it's not going to be a peace deal or necessarily","start":1422},{"end":1440.28,"text":"even a successful nuclear resolution. It's going to be a permanent inflation tax on the global economy","start":1431.26},{"end":1445.42,"text":"that the Iranians have just put in their back pocket because they know they can shut the","start":1440.28},{"end":1451.74,"text":"straight at any time once they've had a chance to rebuild themselves and rebuild their own economy and","start":1445.42},{"end":1453.32,"text":"rebuild their own military supplies.","start":1451.74},{"end":1461.28,"text":"But I think he's changed the right wing propaganda on this. Because when you listen to Fox News or these","start":1453.32},{"end":1467.48,"text":"other people and they just want you to go hard at the Iranians and beat them up like it's a sports team,","start":1461.28},{"end":1474.26,"text":"I think he's now changed the narrative, which is this is a way more complex situation.","start":1467.8799999999999},{"end":1482.22,"text":"And so even the Fox News pundits, they can't seem to get around the fact that the realities are there's","start":1474.26},{"end":1487.6,"text":"just only so much that the Americans can do. And I'll just take you back to the tanker wars in 1984","start":1482.22},{"end":1498.16,"text":"to 1988, very similar situation, very tight parallels where we had to reluctantly escort commercial","start":1487.6},{"end":1503.5400000000002,"text":"shipping out of there. And I think that's where we're going to be. The next president,","start":1498.16},{"end":1509.66,"text":"Cady, will be sitting here saying, okay, well, Trump left me a mess. How do I quietly resolve","start":1503.76},{"end":1515.64,"text":"this with the Iranians so that I can have free flowing oil and goods and services coming through","start":1509.66},{"end":1522.38,"text":"the strait? And that will be done without talking to journalists. That's my prediction. That'll be done","start":1515.64},{"end":1527.9,"text":"in a subterranean sort of a way. Okay. I'm going to take a break and come back and talk about","start":1522.38},{"end":1530.3200000000002,"text":"that extraordinary interview with Meet the Press.","start":1527.9},{"end":1538.3000000000002,"text":"Anthony, you have a few kids. I imagine you're looking forward to Father's Day.","start":1534.3600000000001},{"end":1544.2,"text":"It's a special day in the UK, also here in the US. And look, as a dad myself, I wanted to give our","start":1538.48},{"end":1551.7800000000002,"text":"listeners a quick tip. This year, why not give your dad a membership to the rest of his politics US?","start":1544.2},{"end":1556.28,"text":"And because we love all the dads out there, those who send us questions who are in our founding","start":1551.78},{"end":1559.52,"text":"members club, those who come to our shows and come up and speak to us afterwards,","start":1556.28},{"end":1563.98,"text":"we are offering a 25% discount on an annual gift membership.","start":1559.84},{"end":1570.86,"text":"I mean, come on, Cady, what dad wouldn't want our exclusive founding members Q&A,","start":1564.24},{"end":1577.28,"text":"our members only mini series, ad free listening, early access to our live shows, ticketing,","start":1571.52},{"end":1582.22,"text":"and much, much more. Just head to the rest is politics US.com. You can click on gifts,","start":1577.28},{"end":1586.66,"text":"and the gift will land straight in your dad's inbox right on Father's Day.","start":1582.22},{"end":1592.92,"text":"It's an excellent Father's Day present. I would know. Go to the rest is politics US.com","start":1587},{"end":1598.82,"text":"and click on something to make your dad a founding member. Happy Father's Day, everybody.","start":1592.92},{"end":1600.1399999999999,"text":"Happy Father's Day.","start":1598.82},{"end":1610.9399999999998,"text":"Welcome back to the rest is politics US with me, Anthony Scaramucci and Cady Kay. Cady,","start":1605.8799999999999},{"end":1612.1399999999999,"text":"NBC.","start":1611.4399999999998},{"end":1614.46,"text":"Crooked, crooked NBC.","start":1612.84},{"end":1619.78,"text":"Yes. They spent a lot of money to fly out to Wisconsin. It was an event with farmers out there,","start":1614.46},{"end":1624.92,"text":"and they were trying to get that one-on-one interview with the president. And Kristen","start":1620.08},{"end":1630.92,"text":"Welker was asking him normal questions. I didn't think they were crazy questions. I watched the","start":1624.92},{"end":1635.8,"text":"interview, but he didn't like it. Two things about that interview that I think people should be aware","start":1630.92},{"end":1641.48,"text":"of. Trump threw down the microphone. You could hear him crunching the microphone as he stepped on it.","start":1635.8},{"end":1649.94,"text":"But when he got up, he's not doing squats, Cady Kay. When Trump got up, he was wobbling out of the","start":1642.26},{"end":1655.56,"text":"seat, and he was in an awkward stoop as he walked away. Because they had the boom mics ahead above","start":1649.94},{"end":1659.62,"text":"them. You can't see that in the shot, but they did. I didn't like his mobility. I'll just tell you","start":1655.56},{"end":1665.56,"text":"that. Yeah. That was not a springy guy there. So look, so here's what happens. They do this","start":1659.62},{"end":1670.3999999999999,"text":"interview. It's interrupted by the rain. They keep referring to the rain. Trump is clearly getting","start":1665.56},{"end":1674.8999999999999,"text":"irritated with the rain. Even Trump can't stop the rain, but he wants to do the interview. So he does","start":1670.3999999999999},{"end":1680.12,"text":"the interview. They cover a whole load of things. And then at the end of the interview, they get to the","start":1674.8999999999999},{"start":1680.12,"end":1687.3999999999999,"text":"weaponization fund, and Donald Trump gets into January the 6th and how he would like to help those"},{"start":1687.3999999999999,"end":1694.44,"text":"people because they were ushered in by the FBI. I think anyone who watched the images of January the 6th"},{"start":1694.44,"end":1700.28,"text":"knows that what we saw actually was not FBI agents. And anyway, this was the Trump presidency, not"},{"start":1700.28,"end":1707.96,"text":"the Biden presidency at this point. So it was his FBI. But I did not see FBI agents calmly ushering"},{"start":1707.96,"end":1714.66,"text":"rioters in. What I saw instead was police literally having their heads squeezed between doorways and being"},{"start":1714.66,"end":1721.02,"text":"attacked by these rioters. And from then on, for the next 10 minutes, the whole interview disintegrates,"},{"start":1721.02,"end":1728.34,"text":"and it becomes Kristen Welker doing a good job, I thought, of trying to push the president to produce"},{"start":1728.34,"end":1736.3,"text":"the evidence for his favorite bugbear that the 2020 election was stolen. And there's a back and forth."},{"start":1736.4,"end":1740.82,"text":"And she says, but show me the evidence. There is no evidence. Show us the evidence. There have been"},{"start":1740.82,"end":1748.02,"text":"64 court cases. There was no evidence. He's not buying any of it. And eventually he pulls off his"},{"start":1748.02,"end":1752.36,"text":"microphone and says, okay, I'm done and walks out. He has a history, by the way, of walking out of"},{"start":1752.36,"end":1757.4,"text":"interviews. He's the only person, fun fact here, did you know he was the only person, as far as I"},{"start":1757.4,"end":1762.98,"text":"know, to walk out of an Ali G interview with Sasha Byron Cohen. He kind of got the joke and after a"},{"start":1762.98,"end":1766.96,"text":"few minutes said, okay, I'm done with this. But anyway, he did that. There's a couple of things here"},{"start":1766.96,"end":1771.02,"text":"that I think are worth talking about. One is how do journalists handle Donald Trump?"},{"start":1771.02,"end":1779.16,"text":"When you have the president of the United States on television saying that the 2020 election was"},{"start":1779.16,"end":1787.66,"text":"stolen, saying that the California election count, which is currently being counted, is corrupt and"},{"start":1787.66,"end":1795.54,"text":"crooked. Do you stop the interview? Do you focus on one of the lies and just make sure your audience"},{"start":1795.54,"end":1805.2,"text":"knows it is a lie that the FBI ushered in rioters? It is a lie that the California election process is"},{"start":1805.2,"end":1811.72,"text":"crooked and rigged. It still surprises me. And it's one of the reasons why I'm just never sure if I would"},{"start":1811.72,"end":1817.12,"text":"like to interview Donald Trump, because unless you spend your whole time playing fact checker,"},{"start":1817.44,"end":1824.6,"text":"you're doing your audience a disservice. Because he does. He lies. He is clearly in that"},{"start":1824.6,"end":1829.74,"text":"meet the press interview. What's he actually doing in that interview that matters? It's not so much"},{"start":1829.74,"end":1837.24,"text":"the re-litigation of 2020 or even of January the 6th. He is laying the groundwork for being able to say"},{"start":1837.24,"end":1842.82,"text":"that the California election that's taking place at the moment was stolen. And he's laying the groundwork"},{"start":1842.82,"end":1847.84,"text":"for being able to say the midterm election is stolen if it doesn't go the way I want it."},{"start":1847.84,"end":1855.84,"text":"Give more context. So what's going on in California? And tell us how the president is breeding and feeding"},{"start":1855.84,"end":1857.52,"text":"that sort of uncertainty."},{"start":1858.12,"end":1867.24,"text":"So California has, as their officials would say, a slow count process. You are allowed to have your"},{"start":1867.24,"end":1874.06,"text":"ballot counted in California if you've mailed it in and it is postmarked the day of the election."},{"start":1874.06,"end":1883.02,"text":"So any ballot that arrives into the election houses, counting rooms that is postmarked from the day of"},{"start":1883.02,"end":1888.62,"text":"the election, on the day of the election, that can be counted, even if it arrives four days later."},{"start":1889.46,"end":1894.96,"text":"California mail can be slow. The postal service has been pretty degraded over the last few years,"},{"start":1895.06,"end":1900.94,"text":"so it's even slower. So they are literally still counting. It can take six, seven days. That's the"},{"start":1900.94,"end":1906.8400000000001,"text":"nature of California elections. It will take six, seven days in November because of this system that"},{"start":1906.8400000000001,"end":1911.66,"text":"they have. And Kristen Welkin was saying that to the president. That is the California system. It's rigged."},{"text":"It's crooked. That is the California system. They count the ballots. They count everything that arrives","start":1911.74,"end":1916.94},{"text":"that has been postmarked on the day of the election itself. It takes time. But he doesn't buy it. What he's","start":1916.94,"end":1923.04},{"text":"wanting to say, and he says it with such certainty, it's rigged. It's being stolen is what he's saying.","start":1923.04,"end":1929.88},{"text":"He's setting up a process whereby he can say, if I don't win, and he sees his candidates as proxies","start":1929.88,"end":1936.5800000000002},{"text":"for himself, if I don't win, then the election was stolen. But actually, maybe it's a dumb system.","start":1936.5800000000002,"end":1944.16},{"text":"Maybe you don't like the system. Maybe they shouldn't accept mail-in ballots that arrive","start":1944.3000000000002,"end":1948.7800000000002},{"text":"after election day, and you have to just get your act together and post them two weeks beforehand.","start":1948.7800000000002,"end":1953.3400000000001},{"text":"But that's not the system. So given that we have the system we have, you want to try and do all you","start":1953.34,"end":1960.52},{"text":"can, I think, to make peace sure people believe in the integrity of the system. Because what Donald","start":1960.52,"end":1965.84},{"text":"Trump is doing is undermining public belief in democracy. And that's his goal.","start":1965.84,"end":1971.26},{"text":"He's winning at that. He's been very successful at that because a lot of people","start":1971.76,"end":1975.6599999999999},{"text":"have grown very cynical. And I've said this, it would have almost been better off for America","start":1975.6599999999999,"end":1980.86},{"text":"and the world if he had won in 2020. Because if he won in 2020, he'd already be out to pasture.","start":1980.86,"end":1987.02},{"text":"And we wouldn't have had this insurrection, and we wouldn't have had this electoral stress,","start":1987.2199999999998,"end":1992.06},{"text":"where he's challenging the integrity of all of our elections, and he's making people","start":1992.36,"end":1996.3999999999999},{"text":"feel that there's a quote-unquote rigged system.","start":1996.3999999999999,"end":2000.6},{"text":"But I want to flip back to the journalist for a second, Caddy. Do you remember Bernard Goldberg?","start":2000.6,"end":2006.6},{"text":"Does the name sound familiar? Uh-uh. Okay. So Bernard Goldberg worked at a place called CBS News,","start":2007.4399999999998,"end":2012.8},{"text":"which is considered the Tiffany Network. And he worked for Dan Rather, who was the anchor at CBS","start":2012.9399999999998,"end":2018.08},{"text":"News and many others. And he wrote a book about 15 years ago called Bias, B-I-A-S. And it was his","start":2018.08,"end":2026.3799999999999},{"text":"interpretation that CBS News had a liberal bias to it. And he articulated all the things that he saw","start":2026.38,"end":2034.18},{"text":"at CBS News. It became a nonfiction bestseller in the United States. He went on and did many interviews","start":2034.18,"end":2039.8600000000001},{"text":"about this topic. And obviously, CBS News refuted it, et cetera. But my question to you as a journalist,","start":2039.8600000000001,"end":2048.04},{"text":"someone who has been around for three decades, mainstream news, is it biased, Caddy? Is it","start":2048.04,"end":2056.22},{"text":"tilted to the left? So I would say it's less that it's tilted to the left, Anthony. But I do think,","start":2056.22,"end":2063.4},{"text":"and I've heard this from conservatives for years around America, that they've lost the big institutions","start":2063.64,"end":2070.04},{"text":"of democracy like universities and the press to liberals and that it's skewed against them.","start":2070.04,"end":2077.38},{"text":"And I think in one respect, they're right. And that is that the major newspapers and broadcasters","start":2077.92,"end":2085.2799999999997},{"text":"of the United States have their headquarters in New York and Washington and Los Angeles. They do not","start":2085.2799999999997,"end":2092.4},{"text":"have them in Kansas or Oklahoma or even Texas. And that produces a consensus in the editorial rooms of","start":2092.4,"end":2101.46},{"text":"those media organizations that is generally pro-globalization, pro-elites, pro-education.","start":2101.46,"end":2110.44},{"text":"Just all of those people in those editorial boardrooms tend to come from elite universities,","start":2110.78,"end":2117.98},{"text":"have more of a global flavor. They probably are more likely to lean democratic because we know that","start":2117.98,"end":2127.2400000000002},{"text":"90% of people with university degrees end up in cities and they tend to vote more democratic.","start":2127.2400000000002,"end":2131.8},{"text":"But I think it's as much the coast of America being out of touch with the heartland of America","start":2132.3,"end":2139.22},{"text":"as it is a deliberate bias. Because I do think, having worked in news organizations for a long time,","start":2139.22,"end":2145.3},{"start":2145.3,"end":2152.5800000000004,"text":"that journalists of reputable organizations, by and large, don't always get it right, but they really"},{"start":2152.5800000000004,"end":2159.1200000000003,"text":"try to be fair. I think they have a real commitment to that. Donald Trump does not try to be fair."},{"start":2159.78,"end":2166.28,"text":"In 2016, his rallies were a kind of playbook. It was like the greatest hits. The first 30 minutes would"},{"start":2166.28,"end":2173.2400000000002,"text":"be how wonderful he is. The next 30 minutes would be the press can't be believed they're all rigged."},{"start":2173.24,"end":2177.66,"text":"And the last 30 minutes would be an attack on Hillary Clinton. And it was a deliberate strategy."},{"start":2177.9799999999996,"end":2183,"text":"If you undermine the public's belief in the press, even if the press is doing a good job,"},{"start":2183.14,"end":2188.56,"text":"then you can say that the election was rigged. And you can say to people, don't believe it just"},{"start":2188.56,"end":2194.4399999999996,"text":"because you read it in the press, because the press, after all, is rigged. So I think it's very"},{"start":2194.4399999999996,"end":2200.9799999999996,"text":"cynical what he's doing when he attacks the press. And yes, he's playing on some prejudices and"},{"start":2200.98,"end":2207.16,"text":"beliefs that people have that the press had become skewed against them and didn't represent their"},{"start":2207.16,"end":2212.78,"text":"values. But I think it's also his, he is being far more cynical than that. He is just deciding that"},{"start":2212.78,"end":2218.2,"text":"if I make people distrust the press, then when the press tells them I've lost the election,"},{"start":2218.86,"end":2221.3,"text":"they won't believe the press. They'll believe me."},{"start":2221.84,"end":2226.58,"text":"Scott Pelley, is he liberal? And just for those who may not know who Scott Pelley is,"},{"start":2226.58,"end":2233.34,"text":"he was just recently fired by CBS News. He was one of the lead correspondents on probably their"},{"start":2233.34,"end":2239.9,"text":"most successful news program, 60 Minutes, which had a banner year last year, over $200 million of"},{"start":2239.9,"end":2246.3199999999997,"text":"advertising revenues flowed into 60 Minutes. But he got fired because he wasn't telling the line for"},{"start":2246.3199999999997,"end":2249.2999999999997,"text":"Barry Weiss and some of the other people at 60 Minutes."},{"start":2249.36,"end":2253.6,"text":"Well, and he confronted his boss in a way in a public meeting that, I don't know,"},{"start":2253.6,"end":2256.7999999999997,"text":"I think most people would probably think you're going to have some kind of retribution,"},{"start":2257.02,"end":2258.54,"text":"some kind of comeuppance for doing that."},{"start":2258.64,"end":2259.12,"text":"Is he liberal?"},{"start":2259.8399999999997,"end":2260.2,"text":"Probably."},{"start":2260.66,"end":2260.92,"text":"Okay."},{"start":2261.04,"end":2261.8199999999997,"text":"With a small L."},{"start":2262.2599999999998,"end":2268.38,"text":"So to me, this is the genius of Donald Trump. And people, please pay close attention."},{"start":2269.2799999999997,"end":2273.56,"text":"If I'm looking at it as objectively as possible, and Cady gave you a great answer,"},{"start":2273.56,"end":2280.44,"text":"but I'm not a news person. If I just, on the outside, I would say lowercase L,"},{"start":2280.44,"end":2288.06,"text":"the mainstream media, the big networks, does have a lowercase L liberal bias. Okay. It's just"},{"start":2288.06,"end":2293.64,"text":"not as far to the left as Fox News would tell you or Donald Trump, but it's a lowercase L."},{"start":2294.14,"end":2301.8,"text":"But you see that fact, Cady, allows a lot of space for Donald Trump to maneuver it. Okay. And so"},{"start":2301.8,"end":2304.48,"text":"that's where he gives out this big chant."},{"start":2304.6,"end":2310.1,"text":"But it's not just that. I don't think it's just that, Anthony, because Trump rails against,"},{"start":2310.1,"end":2318.08,"text":"and we've said this before, Trump wants 150% loyalty. 95% loyalty is not enough."},{"start":2318.44,"end":2318.88,"text":"And we know that."},{"start":2318.88,"end":2322.1,"text":"He wants the MyPillow network everywhere, right?"},{"end":2322.88,"text":"Yes.","start":2322.42},{"end":2325.14,"text":"So he will trash Fox News.","start":2322.88},{"end":2325.8399999999997,"text":"Right.","start":2325.6},{"end":2329.7,"text":"If Fox News has one item that doesn't agree with him.","start":2326.06},{"end":2330.16,"text":"Yeah.","start":2329.98},{"end":2336.08,"text":"This is not about whether Americans have a legitimate grievance about being excluded from","start":2330.3199999999997},{"end":2342.14,"text":"liberal institutions. Conservative Americans have a legitimate grievance. This is about loyalty to","start":2336.08},{"end":2348.1,"text":"Donald Trump and nothing else. Otherwise, why does he go after those networks that generally support","start":2342.14},{"end":2348.86,"text":"him, which he does?","start":2348.1},{"end":2354.2200000000003,"text":"I agree with you because his attitude is anybody that's against me, I'm going to obliterate. And","start":2348.86},{"end":2357.76,"text":"he's been doing that successfully for five decades. So I give him credit.","start":2354.2200000000003},{"end":2364.7000000000003,"text":"Cady, do you remember the incident between Dan Rather and George Herbert Walker Bush? It happened","start":2358.42},{"end":2368.28,"text":"38 short years ago. Vaguely remember?","start":2364.7000000000003},{"end":2373.96,"text":"Well, I know. I'm remembering the more recent Dan Rather incident that was the one that got him","start":2369.28},{"end":2380.82,"text":"moved out of CBS News. He told a story about George Walker Bush on the news that was not factually","start":2373.96},{"end":2387.34,"text":"accurate and it wasn't sourced well and it damaged him. But this happened in January 25th, 1988.","start":2380.82},{"end":2393.98,"text":"He's hitting him on the Iran-Contra affair. Okay. I'm bringing this up for a reason. Just stay with me.","start":2387.9},{"end":2400.56,"text":"Bush retorts to him and says, well, you walked off the set for seven minutes, which rather was","start":2394.58},{"end":2405.94,"text":"sometimes petulant, he goes, I wouldn't want your career defined by that seven minute walk-off","start":2400.56},{"end":2412.12,"text":"any more than I want my career defined by the mistakes that we made in that one-off thing called","start":2405.94},{"end":2419.9,"text":"Iran-Contra. And this boosted Bush. This was like, oh my God, Bush isn't a wimp. Look at the way he took","start":2412.12},{"end":2426.96,"text":"on Dan Rather and it helped him get to the nomination for the president. And the reason I'm","start":2419.9},{"end":2436.56,"text":"bringing this up is Donald Trump understands this better than anybody. If you have a journalist","start":2426.96},{"end":2442.44,"text":"that's asking pointed questions, and again, even if those questions are true, and even if they're","start":2436.56},{"end":2450.26,"text":"moderate questions or lowercase l questions, if you press the nuclear bomb on the journalist,","start":2442.44},{"end":2455.54,"text":"the possession bias is towards you, Katty K. Am I right?","start":2451},{"end":2457.58,"text":"Yeah. Because nobody likes journalists.","start":2455.54},{"end":2464.2000000000003,"text":"Okay. So that is the motif that Trump is doing. Don't say that, Katty. You're a journalist. I like","start":2457.7200000000003},{"end":2467.7400000000002,"text":"you. Okay. You're probably everybody's favorite journalist, by the way.","start":2464.2000000000003},{"end":2473.58,"text":"Can I give actually the cynical interpretation of what is happening with CBS News and 60 Minutes?","start":2467.74},{"end":2477,"text":"Please. I'm dying to hear, right? Just try to explain the motif of Trump. But go ahead.","start":2473.66},{"end":2485.2,"text":"Yes. And I think you're right that this has been an easy stream for Donald Trump to mine. I just think","start":2477.12},{"end":2491.4799999999996,"text":"we have to be careful to separate what have been legitimate grievances that some conservatives","start":2485.2},{"end":2495.58,"text":"Americans have had in the heartland of America about not having their voices represented","start":2491.4799999999996},{"end":2502.86,"text":"in the American press from what is a very cynical manipulation of the public to stop believing","start":2495.58},{"end":2510.14,"text":"the press so that you can then say the 2020 election was stolen. January the 6th was a day","start":2502.86},{"end":2516.9,"text":"of patriots walking on the Capitol and being ushered in by the FBI, right? I mean, I think let's not","start":2510.14},{"end":2523.7,"text":"conflate the two things because one of them is a disastrous, if we keep going down that road,","start":2516.9},{"end":2530.12,"text":"it's going to undermine our democracy. I think actually behind all of this is something very","start":2523.7},{"end":2537.3999999999996,"text":"cynical. And this is just a win for Larry Ellison. From his point of view, this is about trying to","start":2530.12},{"end":2548.68,"text":"make sure that Paramount can buy the Warner Brothers, get that deal through. And so he needs, so Barry Weiss","start":2537.3999999999996},{"end":2555.4199999999996,"text":"needs to make him happy and make sure that CBS News looks a little bit more Trump compliant so that","start":2548.68},{"end":2561.7999999999997,"text":"there'll be less regulatory hurdles to that deal. I think this is just about business and making money.","start":2555.4199999999996},{"end":2569.68,"text":"So I have nothing to say there because I think that is the exact right narrative and I'm one in 100%","start":2562.3399999999997},{"end":2574.4199999999996,"text":"agreement with you. In the end, guys, follow the money. Follow the money.","start":2569.68},{"end":2576.88,"text":"How many times we said that on this program? Amen.","start":2574.42},{"end":2583.92,"text":"Before we go, everybody, I want to quickly remind you about our founding members mini series,","start":2577.86},{"end":2586.34,"text":"The Truth About Donald Trump's Health.","start":2584.04},{"end":2594.46,"text":"Katty, Trump turns 80 on Sunday. My God. Okay. Which means we probably have 80 more years to go","start":2587.3},{"end":2599.56,"text":"with Donald Trump. But with that in mind, we thought now would be the right time to discuss whether","start":2594.46},{"end":2604.64,"text":"someone of Trump's old age with rumors flying around about his physical and cognitive health.","start":2599.56},{"end":2607.52,"text":"Is he fit to serve as president of the United States?","start":2604.88},{"end":2611.96,"text":"Yeah. So the second episode comes out this Wednesday. In the first episode,","start":2608.16},{"end":2617.12,"text":"I spoke to physician Dr. Vin Gupta about the president's health. And I think you're going","start":2612.34},{"end":2622.7,"text":"to be shocked by what he had to hear about the president. You might be interested to find out just how well","start":2638.68},{"end":2650.84,"text":"the United States stacks up on the gerontocracy scale.
